title: esenbeckia grandiflora subsp. brevipetiolata
intro: Esenbeckia grandiflora subsp. brevipetiolata, also known as the 'Short-petiolar Esenbeckia', is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and roots of Esenbeckia grandiflora subsp. brevipetiolata are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, thanks to its analgesic, antipyretic, and antibacterial effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and its essential oils are extracted for use in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
